rationale: The citing paper’s core contribution is a psychological mechanism test (imagery-driven implicit attitude change in warnings), whereas the cited paper is an econometric FTC-regulation demand study using market purchase data, so their overlap appears mainly in broad deceptive-advertising/consumer-protection context. The only linkage evidence provided is a single DOI-level matched reference with no in-text citation context. Given this sparse evidence, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only, but substantive use is also not clearly established.
